title: Pi Desktop Notification Extension
context: Pi extension that sends GNOME desktop notifications via notify-send when agent_end fires and terminal is not focused. Uses CSI ? 1004 focus events for focus tracking. Ptyxis (VTE-based) terminal on Linux/GNOME.
architecture: Two modules: focus-tracker (stdin focus event listener) and index.ts (extension glue + notify-send). Focus tracking enables terminal focus reporting via ESC[?1004h, listens for ESC[I (focus in) / ESC[O (focus out) on stdin. Extension hooks agent_end, checks focus state, spawns notify-send if unfocused.

red.description: Write focus-tracker.test.ts with tests for: enableFocusTracking writes ESC[?1004h to stdout, disableFocusTracking writes ESC[?1004l, stdin ESC[I sets focused=true, stdin ESC[O sets focused=false, default state is focused=true, cleanup handler removes listener

green.description: Implement focus-tracker.ts with enable/disable functions, stdin raw mode listener for focus events, isFocused() getter, cleanup() for teardown

red.description: Write notify.test.ts with tests for: spawns notify-send with title and body args, returns silently when notify-send binary not found (ENOENT), escapes body text for shell safety

green.description: Implement notify.ts that uses spawnSync to call notify-send with --icon and title/body args, catches ENOENT gracefully

red.description: Write index.test.ts with tests for: hooks agent_end event, calls notify when unfocused, skips notify when focused, includes turn count in notification body ("Turn N complete"), falls back to always-notify when focus tracking unavailable, hooks session_shutdown for cleanup

green.description: Implement index.ts extension that imports focus-tracker and notify, hooks agent_end to check isFocused() and send notification with turn count from event.messages, hooks session_shutdown to call cleanup()

refactor.description: Extract notification body formatting into a helper function

red.description: Write integration.test.ts with tests for: extension loads without errors, registers agent_end handler via pi.on(), registers session_shutdown handler via pi.on()

green.description: Verify extension calls pi.on for both events, no registerCommand/registerTool needed

design.currentState: No desktop notification extension exists. Pi ships with an example notify.ts in examples/extensions/ that uses terminal escape codes (OSC 777/99) for notifications but has no focus gating. Extensions live in ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ as Bun workspace packages with bun:test for testing.
design.desiredState: A new pi-notify extension in ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ that: (1) tracks terminal focus via CSI ?1004 focus events, (2) on agent_end, sends a GNOME desktop notification via notify-send with turn count context ONLY when terminal is not focused, (3) defaults to always-notify if focus events unsupported, (4) cleans up on session_shutdown.
design.patterns: Integration tests: cheap-clarify/integration.test.ts pattern (mock pi API, verify registerCommand/on calls). Unit tests: co-located *.test.ts with bun:test. Package structure: index.ts + package.json + tsconfig.json extending tsconfig.base.json. Shared mocks from @pi-ext/shared/test-mocks. Workspace registration in root package.json.
design.decisions: Use notify-send for GNOME notifications. Focus tracking via CSI ?1004h stdin listener. Default state = focused (conservative). Three modules: focus-tracker.ts (focus event listener), notify.ts (notify-send wrapper), index.ts (extension glue). Notification includes turn count. No session_start notification.
